title: Efficacy and safety of lanadelumab for long-term prophylaxis of hereditary angioedema: a randomized controlled trial
abstract: This randomized controlled trial evaluated lanadelumab 300mg every 4 weeks versus placebo in 125 patients with HAE type 1/2. The primary endpoint was the mean attack rate. Double-blind placebo-controlled design. SERPING1 mutations confirmed in all patients.
authors: Banerji A; Riedl MA; Bernstein JA
why_it_matters_for_ma: C1-INH欠乏型HAEの長期予防を対象としたRCTとして、有効性・安全性の根拠として医師説明やpayer交渉で活用できる可能性があります。

title: Icatibant versus placebo for acute hereditary angioedema attacks: phase 3 randomized trial
abstract: Phase 3 randomized controlled trial of icatibant (Firazyr) for on-demand treatment of acute HAE attacks. C1-INH deficiency confirmed. Primary endpoint: time to first symptom relief.
journal: Journal of Allergy and Clinical Immunology
why_it_matters_for_ma: C1-INH欠乏型HAEの急性発作治療に関するRCTとして、治療選択の臨床的根拠として医療者への情報提供に活用できる可能性があります。

title: Short-term prophylaxis with C1-INH concentrate before surgical procedures in patients with hereditary angioedema
abstract: Retrospective cohort study of perioperative prophylaxis management in 94 HAE patients. C1 esterase inhibitor deficiency confirmed by complement assay. Perioperative management included C1-INH IV (Berinert) or fresh frozen plasma (FFP) in 12% of cases.
why_it_matters_for_ma: C1-INH欠乏型HAEの短期予防に関するリアルワールドデータとして、周術期管理の実態把握とpayer対応の根拠として活用できる可能性があります。

title: Comparative effectiveness of long-term prophylaxis for hereditary angioedema: systematic review and network meta-analysis
abstract: Network meta-analysis (NMA) of randomized trials assessing lanadelumab, berotralstat, and C1-INH SC (Haegarda) for long-term prophylaxis of HAE. We searched MEDLINE, EMBASE, and Cochrane for relevant studies.
journal: Journal of Allergy and Clinical Immunology
authors: Li Y; Chen X; Wang Q
why_it_matters_for_ma: HAEの治療に関するメタ解析・SRとして、エビデンス総括の根拠資料として活用できる可能性があります。

title: Factor XII-associated hereditary angioedema with normal C1 inhibitor: clinical characteristics and genetic analysis
abstract: Observational study of 45 patients with HAE with normal C1-INH (HAE-nC1INH) carrying factor XII mutations. FXII variants were identified in 38 patients. Estrogen-dependent exacerbation was noted in 22 female patients.
authors: Magerl M; Goedel A; Maurer M

title: International WAO/EAACI guideline for the management of hereditary angioedema: 2023 update
abstract: This clinical practice guideline provides updated treatment recommendations for hereditary angioedema management. Expert consensus from the WAO and EAACI. Evidence-based position statement on acute treatment and long-term prophylaxis.
authors: Maurer M; Magerl M; Betschel S

title: Long-term safety and efficacy of lanadelumab: open-label extension study of the HELP OLE trial
abstract: Open-label extension (OLE) of the HELP randomized controlled trial. Patients with HAE type 1/2 received lanadelumab for up to 30 months. Long-term tolerability data are reported.
authors: Riedl MA; Longhurst HJ; Cicardi M

title: Prevalence and attack frequency of hereditary angioedema: results from the HAE international registry
abstract: Registry study of 1,234 HAE patients across 14 countries. Annual attack rate, prevalence estimates, and hospitalization data are reported. HAE emergency department visits accounted for 38% of all acute episodes.
authors: Longhurst HJ; Aberer W; Bouillet L

title: Patient-reported outcomes and quality of life in hereditary angioedema: systematic review
abstract: Systematic review of patient-reported outcomes (PRO) and QoL instruments used in HAE clinical trials. EQ-5D, HAE-QoL, and SF-36 scores were collected. Disease burden and work productivity impairment (WPAI) were assessed.
why_it_matters_for_ma: HAEに関するメタ解析・SRとして、エビデンスの全体像把握に活用できる可能性があります。

title: Plasma kallikrein-kinin pathway activation in hereditary angioedema: mechanistic insights
abstract: In vitro study examining complement activation and bradykinin pathway in HAE. Animal model (mouse model) of C1-INH deficiency used. Pathogenesis of contact activation system dysregulation.

title: Berotralstat (BCX7353) for prevention of hereditary angioedema attacks: a randomized controlled trial
abstract: Phase 3 randomized controlled trial of berotralstat (Orladeyo) for long-term prophylaxis of HAE attacks. C1-INH deficiency confirmed. 110 patients randomized 1:1 to berotralstat 110mg or placebo.
authors: Zuraw BL; Bernstein JA; Maurer M

title: Garadacimab (CSL312, anti-FXIIa antibody) for long-term prophylaxis of hereditary angioedema: phase 3 trial
abstract: Randomized double-blind placebo-controlled trial of garadacimab for HAE prophylaxis. SERPING1 mutation carriers enrolled. Monthly subcutaneous dosing evaluated.
authors: Zuraw BL; Busse P; Caballero T

title: Real-world effectiveness of lanadelumab for hereditary angioedema prophylaxis: a prospective cohort study
abstract: Prospective cohort study of 210 HAE patients treated with lanadelumab in routine clinical practice. HAE type 1/2 population. Attack rate reduction and safety outcomes reported.
authors: Longhurst H; Cicardi M; Craig T

title: Current concepts in the treatment of hereditary angioedema: a narrative review
abstract: Narrative review of acute and long-term prophylaxis treatment options for hereditary angioedema. Updates on recent clinical trials of lanadelumab, berotralstat, and garadacimab. C1-INH deficiency management is discussed.

title: Case report: hereditary angioedema in a patient with novel SERPING1 mutation presenting with laryngeal edema
abstract: We report a case of HAE type 1 in a 28-year-old woman with a novel SERPING1 variant. C1-INH level was markedly reduced. Acute attack was treated with icatibant (Firazyr) on-demand.

title: Diagnostic delay in hereditary angioedema: role of C4 levels and functional C1 inhibitor assay

authors: Caballero T; Prior N; Perez-Fernandez E

title: Plasminogen mutation-associated hereditary angioedema: clinical phenotype and angioedema mechanism
abstract: Cohort study of patients with hereditary angioedema associated with PLG mutations (HAE-nC1INH). Normal C1 inhibitor levels confirmed. ANGPT1 variants identified in 3 patients. HS3ST6 mutations excluded.
journal: Journal of Allergy and Clinical Immunology

title: Perioperative management of hereditary angioedema patients undergoing major surgery: real-world data
abstract: Retrospective observational study of perioperative prophylaxis in 67 HAE surgeries. Pre-surgical management with C1-INH IV concentrate (Berinert) or FFP used in all cases. Pre-procedural angioedema prophylaxis outcomes are reported.
